questions.hindi: मैं एक कप चाय पीना चाहूँगा।

options.english: I am drinking tea.
options.hindi: मैं चाय पी रहा हूँ।

options.english: I drink tea daily.
options.hindi: मैं रोज़ चाय पीता हूँ।

options.english: I drank tea yesterday.
options.hindi: मैंने कल चाय पी।

options: I would like to have a cup of tea.
questions.answer: I would like to have a cup of tea.
questions.explanation: 'Would like to' = polite 'want to'. Subject + would like to + base verb.

questions.sentence: Would you _____ to see the menu?
questions.answer: like
questions.explanation: 'Would you like to...' is the correct question form. 'Would' is already there, so 'like' follows.

questions.sentence: She would likes to meet the manager.
options: She would likes to meet the manager.
She would like to meet the manager.
questions.answer: She would like to meet the manager.
questions.explanation: After 'would', we use the base form 'like', not 'likes'. Would + like (not likes).

options.english: Do you want to sit here?
options.hindi: क्या तुम यहाँ बैठना चाहते हो?

options.english: Are you sitting here?
options.hindi: क्या तुम यहाँ बैठे हो?

options: Would you like to sit here?

options.english: You should sit here.
options.hindi: तुम्हें यहाँ बैठना चाहिए।

questions.answer: Would you like to sit here.
questions.explanation: Question form: Would + subject + like to + base verb?

questions.sentence: I would like _____ introduce myself.
questions.answer: to
questions.explanation: 'Would like to' — 'to' is mandatory before the base verb. Would like TO + base verb.

questions.hindi: वह manager से मिलना नहीं चाहेगी।
options: She would not like to meet the manager.

options.english: She does not meet the manager.
options.hindi: वह manager से नहीं मिलती।

options.english: She cannot meet the manager.
options.hindi: वह manager से नहीं मिल सकती।

options.english: She did not meet the manager.
options.hindi: वह manager से नहीं मिली।

questions.answer: She would not like to meet the manager.
questions.explanation: Negative: Subject + would not like to + base verb. 'Would not' or 'wouldn't' — both correct.

questions.sentence: We would like to _____ this project by Friday.
questions.answer: finish
questions.explanation: After 'would like to', use BASE VERB (infinitive without 'to'). Never use -ing form here.

questions.sentence: Would she likes to attend the meeting?
options: Would she likes to attend the meeting?
Would she like to attend the meeting?
questions.answer: Would she like to attend the meeting.
questions.explanation: In questions with 'would', always use 'like' (base form), not 'likes'. Would + subject + like to...

questions.hindi: हम आपको यह position offer करना चाहेंगे।

options.english: We offered you this position.
options.hindi: हमने आपको यह position दी थी.

options.english: We are offering you this position.
options.hindi: हम आपको यह position offer कर रहे हैं.

options: We would like to offer you this position.

options.english: We want to give this to you.
options.hindi: हम यह आपको देना चाहते हैं.

questions.answer: We would like to offer you this position.
questions.explanation: 'Would like to' in business/professional context for making offers politely.

questions.sentence: I _____ like to apologize for the delay.
questions.answer: would
questions.explanation: 'Would like to' is the complete expression. 'Would' is the modal verb used here.

questions.sentence: I would like to went to the market.
options: I would like to went to the market.
I would like to go to the market.
questions.answer: I would like to go to the market.
questions.explanation: After 'would like to', always use BASE VERB. 'Go' not 'went' (past tense).

questions.hindi: मैं इस company में अपना career build करना चाहूँगा।

options.english: I want to build my career here.
options.hindi: मैं यहाँ career बनाना चाहता हूँ.

options.english: I am building my career here.
options.hindi: मैं यहाँ career बना रहा हूँ.

options: I would like to build my career in this company.

options.english: I built my career here.
options.hindi: मैंने यहाँ career बनाया.

questions.answer: I would like to build my career in this company.
questions.explanation: 'Would like to' for polite/formal expression of desire. Used in interviews.

questions.sentence: _____ you like to have some coffee?
questions.answer: Would
questions.explanation: Question with 'would like to': Would + subject + like to + base verb?

questions.sentence: Do you would like to come to the party?
options: Do you would like to come to the party?
Would you like to come to the party?
questions.answer: Would you like to come to the party?
questions.explanation: Don't use 'Do' with 'would'. Question: Would + subject + like to...? (not Do/Does)

questions.hindi: वे इस meeting को cancel करना नहीं चाहेंगे।
options: They would not like to cancel this meeting.

options.english: They did not cancel this meeting.
options.hindi: उन्होंने meeting cancel नहीं की.

options.english: They cannot cancel this meeting.
options.hindi: वे meeting cancel नहीं कर सकते.

options.english: They should not cancel this meeting.
options.hindi: उन्हें meeting cancel नहीं करनी चाहिए.

questions.answer: They would not like to cancel this meeting.
questions.explanation: Negative: Subject + would not like to + base verb. 'Wouldn't like to' is the contracted form.

questions.sentence: She would like to _____ her skills further.
questions.answer: develop
questions.explanation: BASE VERB after 'would like to'. 'Develop' — no -ing, no -ed, no -s.

questions.hindi: क्या आप हमारी नई product try करना चाहेंगे?

options.english: Do you want to try our product?
options.hindi: क्या आप हमारी product try करना चाहते हैं?

options.english: Are you trying our product?
options.hindi: क्या आप हमारी product try कर रहे हैं?

options: Would you like to try our new product?

options.english: Have you tried our product?
options.hindi: क्या आपने हमारी product try की है?

questions.answer: Would you like to try our new product?
questions.explanation: Customer service question: Would you like to + base verb?

questions.sentence: I wouldn't like to _____ this opportunity.
questions.answer: miss
questions.explanation: After 'wouldn't like to', use BASE VERB. 'Miss' not 'missed/missing/misses'.

questions.sentence: I would like eating pizza for dinner.
options: I would like eating pizza for dinner.
I would like to eat pizza for dinner.
questions.answer: I would like to eat pizza for dinner.
questions.explanation: 'Would like to + BASE VERB', not 'would like + gerund (-ing)'. Always 'I would like TO eat.'

questions.hindi: मैं अपनी team को motivate करना चाहूँगा।
options: I would like to motivate my team.

options.english: I motivated my team.
options.hindi: मैंने अपनी team को motivate किया.

options.english: I am motivating my team.
options.hindi: मैं अपनी team को motivate कर रहा हूँ.

options.english: I must motivate my team.
options.hindi: मुझे अपनी team को motivate करना है.

questions.answer: I would like to motivate my team.
questions.explanation: 'Would like to' for stating a polite desire/intention.

questions.sentence: We would like to _____ you to our annual event.
questions.answer: invite
questions.explanation: BASE VERB after 'would like to'. 'Invite' — base form.

questions.sentence: Would they likes to join the project?
options: Would they likes to join the project?
Would they like to join the project?
questions.answer: Would they like to join the project?
questions.explanation: After 'would', always BASE FORM 'like', never 'likes'. Would + subject + like to...?

questions.hindi: हम आपके business के साथ collaborate करना चाहेंगे।

options.english: We collaborate with your business.
options.hindi: हम आपके business के साथ collaborate करते हैं.

options.english: We collaborated with your business.
options.hindi: हमने आपके business के साथ collaborate किया.

options: We would like to collaborate with your business.

options.english: We will collaborate with your business.
options.hindi: हम आपके business के साथ collaborate करेंगे.

questions.answer: We would like to collaborate with your business.
questions.explanation: Business proposition using polite 'would like to'.

questions.sentence: I would like to _____ myself as a quick learner.
questions.answer: describe
questions.explanation: BASE VERB after 'would like to'. Used in self-introduction during interviews.

questions.sentence: I am liking to go to the conference.
options: I am liking to go to the conference.
I would like to go to the conference.
questions.answer: I would like to go to the conference.
questions.explanation: 'Would like to' cannot be expressed as 'am liking to'. It's a fixed modal expression.

questions.hindi: क्या आप एक नया account खोलना चाहेंगे?
options: Would you like to open a new account?

options.english: Do you want a new account?
options.hindi: क्या आप नया account चाहते हैं?

options.english: Are you opening a new account?
options.hindi: क्या आप नया account खोल रहे हैं?

options.english: Did you open a new account?
options.hindi: क्या आपने नया account खोला?

questions.answer: Would you like to open a new account?
questions.explanation: Formal/customer service question form.

questions.sentence: He said he would like to _____ the team.
questions.answer: lead
questions.explanation: Indirect speech: 'would like to + BASE VERB'. 'Lead' — base form.

questions.sentence: She would like to speaks at the conference.
options: She would like to speaks at the conference.
She would like to speak at the conference.
questions.answer: She would like to speak at the conference.
questions.explanation: After 'would like to', use BASE VERB — 'speak', not 'speaks'.

questions.hindi: मैं अपना परिचय देना चाहूँगा — मेरा नाम अर्जुन है।

options.english: Let me introduce — my name is Arjun.
options.hindi: मुझे introduce करने दो — मेरा नाम अर्जुन है.

options: I would like to introduce myself — my name is Arjun.

options.english: I am Arjun and I introduce myself.
options.hindi: मैं अर्जुन हूँ और परिचय देता हूँ.

options.english: My name is Arjun and I introduced myself.
options.hindi: मेरा नाम अर्जुन है और मैंने खुद को introduce किया.

questions.answer: I would like to introduce myself — my name is Arjun.
questions.explanation: Classic interview opening using 'would like to introduce myself'.

questions.sentence: Would you like to _____ a tour of our facilities?
questions.answer: take
questions.explanation: BASE VERB after 'would like to'. 'Take a tour' = standard phrase.

questions.sentence: I would not like to missing this class.
options: I would not like to missing this class.
I would not like to miss this class.
questions.answer: I would not like to miss this class.
questions.explanation: After 'would not like to', use BASE VERB. 'Miss', not 'missing'.

questions.hindi: वह HR team से बात करना चाहेगा।

options.english: He talks to HR.
options.hindi: वह HR से बात करता है.

options.english: He talked to HR.
options.hindi: वह HR से बात की.

options: He would like to speak to the HR team.

options.english: He will talk to HR.
options.hindi: वह HR से बात करेगा.

questions.answer: He would like to speak to the HR team.
questions.explanation: Polite professional request using 'would like to'.

questions.sentence: They would like to _____ their business internationally.
questions.answer: expand
questions.explanation: BASE VERB after 'would like to'. 'Expand' — base form.

questions.sentence: Would you like joining our team?
options: Would you like joining our team?
Would you like to join our team?
questions.answer: Would you like to join our team?
questions.explanation: 'Would you like TO + BASE VERB' — not 'would you like + ing'. Always use 'to' + base verb.

questions.hindi: मैं इस काम में पूरी तरह प्रतिबद्ध रहना चाहूँगा।

options.english: I am committed to this work.
options.hindi: मैं इस काम के प्रति प्रतिबद्ध हूँ.

options.english: I was committed to this work.
options.hindi: मैं इस काम के प्रति प्रतिबद्ध था.

options: I would like to remain fully committed to this work.

options.english: I should commit to this work.
options.hindi: मुझे इस काम के प्रति प्रतिबद्ध होना चाहिए.

questions.answer: I would like to remain fully committed to this work.
questions.explanation: Advanced use of 'would like to' with an adjective complement — 'remain + adjective'.
